input_dict.agent_role: Integrate second-hop evidence with first-hop context and retain the facts needed to answer the question.
input_dict.base_prompt: Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

delta_p.trigger: If the context names a candidate film title and the passages contain both (a) a statement that the stage musical or film 'Whistle Down the Wind' is based on a 1959 novel by Mary Hayley Bell (or that the 1961 film is based on that novel) AND (b) a passage saying Ashley Lloyd performed in 'Whistle Down the Wind'.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the agent's existing behavior of identifying and outputting the film title as the answer and retain any accurate facts already extracted from the passages (film title, novel author/year, Ashley Lloyd involvement). Maintain the same concise summary format currently produced.
delta_p.change_rule: When the trigger condition is met, append exactly one brief, integrated sentence to the summary that links the second-hop evidence to the identified film. Concretely, state that the stage musical/production 'Whistle Down the Wind' is based on the film (whose source novel was written by Mary Hayley Bell in 1959) and that Ashley Lloyd performed in that musical, thereby connecting Ashley Lloyd to the film. Use only facts present in the provided passages; do not introduce new facts or speculative details; avoid verbatim long quotes from passages.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not add unrelated background (other songs, producers, or cast members not cited in the passages), do not invent roles/dates not in the passages, and avoid long literal spans copied from the passages. Do not change the answer selection or ask follow-up questions.
delta_p.output_contract: Produce a summary of at most two sentences: (1) identify the film title (use the context film name exactly as given), and (2) a single concise sentence integrating second-hop evidence that the musical/production based on that film/novel links Ashley Lloyd to the film, mentioning Mary Hayley Bell and the 1959 novel when supported by passages. Keep the summary factual and minimal.
delta_p.why_local_transition: This change only augments the existing summary with one connecting sentence so the agent fulfills its role of integrating second-hop evidence without altering how the answer is selected; it is a small, local behavioral addition specific to summary2's responsibility.

visible_agent_inputs.question: Which film based on a novel written by Mary Hayley Bell in 1959, had cast member Ashley Lloyd?
passages: Ashley Lloyd | Ashley Lloyd (billed as Ashley Luke Lloyd) (born (1991--)6 1991 ) is a British actor and dancer, who has performed in hit musical theatre shows such as "Billy Elliot the Musical" and "Whistle Down the Wind".  In 2012 Lloyd appeared in the new musical "Loserville" at the Garrick Theatre in London.  Lloyd is now appearing in the Original London Cast of Dreamgirls at The Savoy Theatre

Whistle Down the Wind (musical) | Whistle Down the Wind is a musical with music composed by Andrew Lloyd Webber, who also co-wrote its book with Patricia Knop and Gale Edwards, and its lyrics were written by Jim Steinman.  It is based on the 1961 film "Whistle Down the Wind", whose source novel of the same name was written by Mary Hayley Bell in 1959.

Whistle Down the Wind (film) | Whistle Down the Wind is a 1961 British film, directed by Bryan Forbes and with screenplay by Keith Waterhouse and Willis Hall.  The film is based on the 1959 novel of the same name by Mary Hayley Bell and stars her daughter, Hayley Mills.
Whistle Down the Wind (1989 musical) | Whistle Down the Wind is a musical based on the novel by Mary Hayley Bell, adapted by Richard Taylor and Russell Labey, with music and lyrics by Richard Taylor.

No Matter What (Boyzone song) | "No Matter What" is a song from the 1996 musical "Whistle Down the Wind" and popularised by Irish boyband Boyzone in 1998 when they recorded it to tie in with the show's first UK production.  The recording was written and produced by Andrew Lloyd Webber, Jim Steinman, Nigel Wright with additional production by Franglen & Lupino.  The song was also featured on the US edition of the soundtrack to the 1999 film "Notting Hill", and was released to American radio on 10 May 1999.

Whistle Down the Wind (novel) | Whistle Down the Wind was a novella written by Mary Hayley Bell and illustrated by Owen Edwards.  It was published in 1959 by Boardman and retailed for 12 shillings and sixpence (62½p).  The central characters are three children — Swallow, Brat and Poor Baby.  The story of the Great Secret is told by Brat.

A Timeless Evening with Sarah Brightman | A Timeless Evening With Sarah Brightman was the first concert tour by English soprano Sarah Brightman accompanied by the London Symphony Orchestra.  The repertoire performed by Brightman was a balanced combination between well-known Classical and Broadway pieces by composers such as Puccini, Gershwin, Delibes, Bernstein and Andrew Lloyd Webber.  Lloyd Webber joined Brightman on stage to perform together the song "Whistle Down the Wind".  Italian tenor Andrea Bocelli sang "Time to Say Goodbye" in a duet with Brightman.

input_dict.output_requirement: Return one summary_2 string integrating only the visible summary_1 and hop2 passages.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are executing one agent in a fixed HotpotQA multi-agent pipeline.

Use the unchanged base instruction p together with the complete ORDERED
list of local prompt deltas. The deltas are supplemental behavior guidance
for this sample; they are not outputs and must not be quoted mechanically.

This is a direct delta-context execution probe:
- Do not rewrite, aggregate, or synthesize a new prompt p'.
- Apply every delta in order to the visible inputs.
- Judge success only by producing the endpoint agent output.
- Do not attempt to reproduce or discuss intermediate states.
- Do not infer hidden information from the existence of the deltas.
- Use only the visible agent inputs as factual evidence.
- Never output gold/oracle labels, evaluation metrics, or hidden support.
- Preserve the agent's normal input/output contract.

Agent:
summary2

Integrate second-hop evidence with first-hop context and retain the facts needed to answer the question.

      "preserve_rule": "Keep the agent's existing behavior of identifying and outputting the film title as the answer and retain any accurate facts already extracted from the passages (film title, novel author/year, Ashley Lloyd involvement). Maintain the same concise summary format currently produced.",

Use only facts present in the provided passages; do not introduce new facts or speculative details; avoid verbatim long quotes from passages.",

Keep the summary factual and minimal.",
      "why_local_transition": "This change only augments the existing summary with one connecting sentence so the agent fulfills its role of integrating second-hop evidence without altering how the answer is selected; it is a small, local behavioral addition specific to summary2's responsibility."
